records.ety: [Prob. from Norw. singl, singling, coarse gravel, small round stones.]

defs: Round, water-worn, and loose gravel and pebbles, or a collection of roundish stones, such as are common on the seashore and elsewhere.

records.ety: [OE. shingle, shindle, fr. L. scindula, scandula; cf. scindere to cleave, to split, E. shed, v. t., Gr. <?/<?/<?/, <?/<?/<?/, shingle, <?/<?/<?/ to slit.]

defs: A piece of wood sawed or rived thin and small, with one end thinner than the other, -- used in covering buildings, especially roofs, the thick ends of one row overlapping the thin ends of the row below.
quotes: I reached St. Asaph, . . . where there is a very poor cathedral church covered with shingles or tiles.

defs: A sign for an office or a shop; as, to hang out one's shingle.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shingled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shingling (?).]

defs: To cover with shingles; as, to shingle a roof.
quotes: They shingle their houses with it.

defs: To cut, as hair, so that the ends are evenly exposed all over the head, as shingles on a roof.
To subject to the process of shindling, as a mass of iron from the pudding furnace.
